stemHtml: During a flight over the Alps, an aircraft experiences a sudden loss of altitude and a significant decrease in airspeed. Which of the following answers is the most correct?

options.a: If the pilot also experiences moderate or severe icing, he/she must make a special air report to ATC. Turbulence alone does NOT need to be reported.
options.b: The pilot is experiencing turbulence but must only make a special air report to ATC in case the turbulence is severe.
options.c: The pilot is experiencing severe mountain waves and must make a special air report to ATC of this as soon as practicable.
options.d: The pilot is experiencing mountain waves and must report this to the meteorological office at the destination airport after landing.
